svg.text: Linux Software Stack — Complete Boot Flow
Power-ON › BootROM › SPL › U-Boot › Linux Kernel › User Space
B O O T S E Q U E N C E ↑
systemd / init (PID 1) · glibc / libc · Shell · Login · Daemons
Your application runs here · Cannot touch hardware directly · Must use system calls to request kernel services
— read · write · open · mmap · ioctl · socket · fork · exec
Hard boundary — user code cannot access kernel memory. All hardware access goes through syscalls.
DEVICE DRIVERS (kernel space — loaded at boot or on demand as modules)
drivers/iio/ · drivers/scsi/ · sound/soc/ · drivers/gpu/drm/ · drivers/mfd/ · drivers/power/ · drivers/soc/
arch/arm64/ · arch/x86/ · arch/riscv/ · CPU feature detection · Interrupt controller (GIC / APIC)
MMU and page table setup · SMP: bring up secondary CPU cores · First kernel code runs at entry point passed from U-Boot
▲ KERNEL ENTRY POINT — U-Boot jumps to kernel here ▲
Runs from DDR RAM · Full shell: "Hit any key to stop autoboot" · ~500 KB – 2 MB in size · YOUR U-Boot patches live here
Fits in internal SRAM (32–64 KB — before DDR RAM is initialized) · Part of U-Boot source tree (common/spl/)
Initializes DDR RAM controller · Loads full U-Boot image from eMMC/SD/NOR Flash into DDR · Jumps to U-Boot entry point
Minimal driver support — only what is needed to find and load U-Boot
ROM / BootROM (burned into chip at factory — cannot be changed)
First code that runs on power-ON · Reads OTP / eFuses to find boot device (eMMC, SD, USB, network)
Validates SPL signature for secure boot chain of trust · Loads SPL into internal SRAM · Starts CPU at EL3 (ARM highest privilege)
CPU cores (ARM Cortex-A, x86, RISC-V) · DDR RAM (LPDDR4/5) · eMMC / SD Card / NOR Flash · PMU
PCIe · USB · Ethernet · I2C · SPI · UART · GPIO · Clock generators · Interrupt controller (GIC/APIC)
DTB = Device Tree Blob — binary file describing hardware topology, passed from U-Boot to kernel in CPU register x0 (arm64)
= ARM Exception Level 3 (highest privilege, secure world) ·
= Md Shofiqul Islam's merged kernel patches (14 patches, 9 subsystems)
